original_poem:
नकोशी  
जगण्याच्या उंबरठ्यावर  
नकोशी जन्मली,  
उजाडून गेलेला तो काळोख  
सर्वत्र नाचत राहतोय.
translated_poem:
Unwanted,  
Born at the threshold of life,  
The darkness that dawned  
Keeps dancing  
Everywhere.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

translated_lines: Born at the threshold of life,

emotional_arc.value: Opens with the stark social fact of an unwanted girl child's birth and deepens into pervasive, unresolved darkness that continues to spread without end.

translated_lines: Born at the threshold of life,

loss_note.value: 'नकोशी' carries the specific Marathi social practice of naming unwanted girl children; 'Unwanted' loses this cultural weight.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: the precarious moment of entry into a life already marked for rejection
a darkness that does not lift even as day breaks, signifying the persistence of social stigma after birth
that darkness keeps actively spreading everywhere

visual_motifs.motif: a newborn girl child lying at a threshold
an unwanted girl child at the threshold of life
darkness spreading and dancing across an open landscape at dawn

translation_note.value: 'Nakoshi' is a Marathi social practice of naming unwanted girl children to signal parental rejection; 'Unwanted' omits this named custom.

stanzas.loss_note: The term Nakoshi carries deep social weight in Maharashtra referring specifically to unwanted female infants which the literal translation Unwanted fails to fully capture.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: the very beginning of existence or birth
the sorrow and gloom that replaces the expected joy of a new birth

cultural_entities.translation_note: Translated literally as Unwanted, losing the specific Maharashtrian cultural context of naming unwanted female children Nakoshi.

gemini-3-flash.raw_text: {"recitation_style":"lament","emotional_arc":"grief","stanzas":[{"index":1,"emotion":"grief","tone":"lament","translation_quality":"faithful","loss_note":"","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"जगण्याच्या उंबरठ्यावर","abstract_meaning":"The moment of birth and the entry into human existence."},{"source_term":"उजाडून गेलेला तो काळोख","abstract_meaning":"The irony of a birth that brings social gloom instead of light."},{"source_term":"काळोख सर्वत्र नाचत राहतोय","abstract_meaning":"The pervasive and mocking nature of social rejection and despair."}],"visual_motifs":["A child at a threshold","Dancing shadows of darkness"]}],"cultural_entities":[{"term":"नकोशी","romanization":"Nakoshi","category":"SOCIAL_CUSTOM","stanza_index":1,"preserved":true,"translation_note":"A name traditionally given to unwanted girl children in Maharashtra, reflecting a social custom rooted in gender bias where the name itself signifies 'unwanted'."}]}

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: The moment of birth and the entry into human existence.
The irony of a birth that brings social gloom instead of light.
The pervasive and mocking nature of social rejection and despair.

cultural_entities.translation_note: A name traditionally given to unwanted girl children in Maharashtra, reflecting a social custom rooted in gender bias where the name itself signifies 'unwanted'.
